title: Active Output Selection Strategies for Multiple Learning Regression Models

sections.title: Previous work
sections.content: Covers active learning in general and for regression tasks, including prior works: [Cohn, 1996] (optimal experimental design), [Settles, 2009] (overview of active learning), [Sugiyama and Rubens, 2008] (multiple models for same task), [Cai et al., 2013] (EMCM for gradient boosted trees), [Cai et al., 2017] (EMCM for GP regression), [Park and Kim, 2020] (robust EMCM), [Zhang et al., 2016] (multiple-output GPs).

sections.title: Active Output Selection (AOS) Introduction
sections.content: Focuses on active learning specialties in calibration context and introduces AOS as a new task.

sections.title: Approaches and New Strategy
sections.content: Describes analyzed approaches, presents new AOS strategy using cross-validation error for leading output selection, and details evaluation methods (toy examples and benchmark dataset).

sections.title: Experimental Details and Results
sections.content: Presents experimental setup, results, and discussion.

sections.title: Conclusion and Future Work
sections.content: Summarizes results and outlines potential future research directions.

key_contributions: Proposes new learning strategy for multiple regression models using cross-validation error to select leading output.
Compares new strategy against existing methods (sequential, round-robin).
Introduces Active Output Selection (AOS) as a novel task for calibration contexts.

previous_works_mentioned.focus: Sequential vs. round-robin strategies for drivability calibration.
Active learning as optimal experimental design.
Overview of active learning state-of-the-art and future work.
Active learning for multiple models in same task.
EMCM for gradient boosted decision trees.
EMCM extension for GP regression and informative queries.
Multiple-output GPs improving single-output prediction via auxiliary outputs.

evaluation_methods: Toy examples with controlled noise and complexity.

new_task.name: Active Output Selection (AOS)
new_task.description: Active learning task for calibration contexts requiring simultaneous identification of multiple process outputs.
